flag_fact: The eagle on Mexico's flag is eating a snake — from an ancient Aztec legend about where to found a great city!
South Africa's flag has 6 colors and a unique Y-shape — no other flag in the world looks like it! It represents unity of the people.
Korea's flag shows the yin-yang symbol and 4 ancient trigrams representing sky, water, fire, and earth!
The Czech flag's distinctive blue triangle was added in 1920 — before that it was just plain red and white stripes.
Canada's red maple leaf flag only became official in 1965 — before that, Canada used the British Union Jack!
The triangle on Bosnia's flag represents the three main communities. The stars along the edge are infinite — they're cut off at both ends!
Qatar's flag is maroon (not red!) with a serrated edge — it's the only national flag in the world with 9 points on its side.
Switzerland has one of only TWO square flags in the world (Vatican City is the other). The white cross on red dates back to the 1300s!
The stars on Brazil's flag show the exact position of stars in the sky on November 15 1889 — the night Brazil became a republic!
Morocco's green Star of Solomon in the center is one of the oldest Islamic symbols — it has been used for centuries.
Haitian revolutionaries tore the white strip from a French flag and sewed blue and red together — creating their own flag to represent freedom!
Scotland's white X on blue (Saint Andrew's Cross) may be the oldest national flag in Europe — legend says it dates back to 832 AD!
The US flag has 50 stars (one per state) and 13 stripes (for the original colonies). It has been redesigned 27 times as new states joined!
Paraguay's flag is the ONLY national flag in the world that has different emblems on the front and back — totally unique!
Australia's flag has the Union Jack the Southern Cross constellation AND a star with 7 points for its 6 states and territories!
The Turkish crescent and star symbol is over 2000 years old — it is one of the most recognized symbols in the entire world.
Germany's black red and gold have been national colors since the 1800s — they represented freedom fighters against oppression!
Curaçao's flag has two yellow stars representing the main island and tiny Klein Curaçao — a beautiful little uninhabited island.
Ivory Coast's orange white and green flag is similar to Ireland's but in reverse order — orange for savannas white for peace green for forests!
Ecuador shares its yellow blue and red colors with Colombia and Venezuela — they were once all one country called Gran Colombia!
The original Dutch flag was orange white and blue — but the orange kept fading to red in the sun so the red version became official!
Japan's flag is one of the simplest in the world — a red circle (the sun) on white. Japan is called the Land of the Rising Sun!
Sweden's gold cross on blue comes from a legend — in 1157 AD a golden cross appeared in the sky before a great battle!
Tunisia's crescent and star design dates back to the Ottoman Empire — Tunisia has used versions of this symbol for many centuries.
Belgium's black yellow and red came from the medieval Duchy of Brabant — a lion on an ancient flag inspired the national colors!
Egypt's golden Eagle of Saladin on the flag is named after the famous medieval Muslim leader who stood against the Crusaders!
Iran's flag has a complex emblem that combines a sword a tulip and the word Allah all in one beautiful design.
New Zealanders voted in a 2016 referendum on whether to change the flag — they chose to keep the Union Jack and Southern Cross design!
Spain's coat of arms includes a castle a lion and a pomegranate — the pomegranate is for Granada the last Moorish city reconquered in 1492!
Cape Verde's 10 yellow stars represent its 10 main islands scattered across the Atlantic Ocean off the coast of West Africa.
Saudi Arabia's flag is the only national flag with writing (the Islamic declaration of faith) AND a sword — both represent deeply held values.
Uruguay's Sun of May represents the sun that appeared through the clouds when independence was declared — the same symbol Argentina uses!
France's blue white and red tricolor became famous during the Revolution in 1789 — it inspired dozens of other countries to use three stripes!
Senegal's green star in the middle comes from the Pan-African flag movement — green represents hope and the forests of Africa.
Norway's Nordic cross appears in all five Scandinavian country flags — it is a shared symbol across the entire Nordic region!
Iraq's flag has the phrase Allahu Akbar written in Arabic script across the middle — it was added during a period of great national pride.
Argentina's light blue and white were chosen by independence hero Manuel Belgrano. The Sun of May celebrates the May 1810 revolution!
Algeria's green crescent and star on green and white have been Algeria's symbol since independence from France in 1962.
Austria's red-white-red flag is one of the oldest national flag designs — legend says it dates back to a battle in 1191 AD!
Jordan's flag has a seven-pointed star representing the seven verses of the Quran's opening AND the seven hills of Amman the capital city!
Portugal's complex coat of arms shows five blue shields for five defeated kings and seven castles — one of the world's most detailed flags!
DR Congo's diagonal red stripe represents the blood of the country's martyrs — red blue and yellow stand for fire peace and wealth!
Uzbekistan's light blue specifically honors the legacy of the Timurid dynasty — the rulers who built the stunning city of Samarkand!
Colombia shares its yellow blue and red with Ecuador and Venezuela — all were once the republic of Gran Colombia! Yellow is for gold.
England's Saint George's Cross (red cross on white) dates back to the Crusades in the 1200s — it is one of Europe's oldest flag designs!
Croatia's distinctive red and white chessboard pattern has been the Croatian national symbol for over 1000 years!
Ghana's red gold and green are Pan-African colors. The black star is the Star of Black Emancipation — Ghana was first to win independence in 1957!
Panama's four-colored flag represents the two main political parties (red and blue) with white squares representing peace between them!
